This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-03-30T19:40:44.4415966Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-30T19:40:29.795422651Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57586,\"title\":\"[Developer Experience] Validation should include analyzer suppressions\",\"body\":\"# Summary\\n\\nAs part of code validation, we should include information about analyzer suppressions.   Currently, we have two categories of analyzers, those that should never be suppressed and those that are legitimate to suppress with architect approval.   \\n\\n## Scope\\n\\nWe should extend the code validation script and Azure SDK CLI experience to offer feedback on both of these, blocking on invalid suppressions and offering warnings that the Azure SDK team should be engaged for PR review of potentially valid suppressions.\\n\\nTo do so, we\\u0026#39;ll need to categorize all of the analyzers - custom and .NET built-ins - running in our repository.\\n\\n### Scenario: Invalid suppressions\\n\\n- Analyzers that should never be suppressed, such as:\\n  -  The file header being present\\n  - Never call `ConfigureAwait(true)` _(AZC0101)_\\n  \\n- Analyzers should not be globally suppressed in the `.csproj`.    They should be suppressed in a specific scope whether using `#pragma`, the `[SuppressMessage]` attribute, or a suppressions file.\\n\\n### Scenario: Potentially valid suppressions\\n\\n- Analyzers that may not be applicable to all libraries, such as:\\n  - Improper model name suffix  _(AZC0030, AZC0031, AZC0033)_\\n  - You must have async and sync overloads  _(AZC0004)_\\n\\nFor these, we should:\\n  - Have the code checks script and Azure SDK CLI output a set of warnings that says \\u0026#34;you may not be able to release\\u0026#34; and suggest engaging with our team - not necessarily the architects.   We can provide first-level guidance.\\n\\n  - If we see these in a pull request, either force our team as reviewers or at least mention us in a comment so that we can engage and provide guidance.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57586\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"assignees\":[\"m-redding\"],\"milestone\":\"Backlog\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-30T19:38:33Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-30T19:38:55Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-30T19:40:44.4434526Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-30T19:40:29.995784642Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57586,\"title\":\"[Developer Experience] Validation should include analyzer suppressions\",\"body\":\"# Summary\\n\\nAs part of code validation, we should include information about analyzer suppressions.   Currently, we have two categories of analyzers, those that should never be suppressed and those that are legitimate to suppress with architect approval.   \\n\\n## Scope\\n\\nWe should extend the code validation script and Azure SDK CLI experience to offer feedback on both of these, blocking on invalid suppressions and offering warnings that the Azure SDK team should be engaged for PR review of potentially valid suppressions.\\n\\nTo do so, we\\u0026#39;ll need to categorize all of the analyzers - custom and .NET built-ins - running in our repository.\\n\\n### Scenario: Invalid suppressions\\n\\n- Analyzers that should never be suppressed, such as:\\n  -  The file header being present\\n  - Never call `ConfigureAwait(true)` _(AZC0101)_\\n  \\n- Analyzers should not be globally suppressed in the `.csproj`.    They should be suppressed in a specific scope whether using `#pragma`, the `[SuppressMessage]` attribute, or a suppressions file.\\n\\n### Scenario: Potentially valid suppressions\\n\\n- Analyzers that may not be applicable to all libraries, such as:\\n  - Improper model name suffix  _(AZC0030, AZC0031, AZC0033)_\\n  - You must have async and sync overloads  _(AZC0004)_\\n\\nFor these, we should:\\n  - Have the code checks script and Azure SDK CLI output a set of warnings that says \\u0026#34;you may not be able to release\\u0026#34; and suggest engaging with our team - not necessarily the architects.   We can provide first-level guidance.\\n\\n  - If we see these in a pull request, either force our team as reviewers or at least mention us in a comment so that we can engage and provide guidance.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57586\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"assignees\":[\"m-redding\"],\"milestone\":\"Backlog\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-30T19:38:33Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-30T19:38:55Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
